reasons_not_useful: phi is CONSTANT over the SdS arc at Lambda=0.01: all r_b ∈ (nan, nan) give floor(r_b)=same integer.
phi takes only 0 distinct value(s) over the arc: []. Cannot be a useful state variable.
Information content of phi is very low: entropy = 0.000 bits. (Maximum possible = log2(0) = 0.00 bits)
phi is CONSTANT over the SdS arc at Lambda=0.1: all r_b ∈ (0.550, 1.801) give floor(r_b)=same integer.
phi takes only 1 distinct value(s) over the arc: [0]. Cannot be a useful state variable.
phi is unit-dependent: rescaling r by 2 changes phi values. This makes phi physically meaningless — it depends on arbitrary unit choices.
Information content of phi is very low: entropy = -0.000 bits. (Maximum possible = log2(1) = 0.00 bits)
phi is CONSTANT over the SdS arc at Lambda=1.0: all r_b ∈ (0.017, 0.995) give floor(r_b)=same integer.
phi takes only 1 distinct value(s) over the arc: [0]. Cannot be a useful state variable.
Information content of phi is very low: entropy = -0.000 bits. (Maximum possible = log2(1) = 0.00 bits)
phi is CONSTANT over the SdS arc at Lambda=10.0: all r_b ∈ (0.001, 0.021) give floor(r_b)=same integer.
phi takes only 1 distinct value(s) over the arc: [0]. Cannot be a useful state variable.

phi_as_state_var.assessment: phi adds NO independent information — collapses to constant or near-constant

phi_attractor_test.assessment: phi coupling does NOT shift the attractor — phi is ineffective as a driver

reasons: Audit across Lambda values: all show ≤2 distinct phi values over SdS arc
phi adds no independent state information
phi coupling does not shift attractor
No physical coupling to SdS metric or thermodynamics
